rules.phrase: We can basically guarantee the income
rules.why: Stayful does not offer guaranteed rent. Making this claim creates false expectations and sets up a trust failure. See entry 036 (betrayal-damaged) for the consequence.
rules.alternative: We can show you what comparable properties earned in their worst month, which gives you a realistic floor to plan from.

rules.phrase: Income is always consistent / We never have quiet months
rules.why: STL income is seasonal. Claiming otherwise is demonstrably false and will destroy trust at first winter payout. Certainty-seekers in particular will hold this against you.
rules.alternative: January-March are quieter — here's what that actually looks like in numbers for your property type.

rules.phrase: [Competitor name] is rubbish / always underperforms / is a bad company
rules.why: Directly criticising named competitors is unprofessional and backfires. The lead chose that company originally — insulting it insults their judgment.
rules.alternative: I won't speak to how they run their business. What I can tell you is exactly how we handle [the specific thing that went wrong].

rules.phrase: It's just 6 months, don't worry about it
rules.why: Minimising the contract term concern without explaining it creates distrust. The lead has a specific anxiety — dismissing it doesn't resolve it.
rules.alternative: The 6 months runs from signing, not from go-live. After that it's 3 months' rolling notice — full flexibility.

rules.phrase: STL is always better than LTL
rules.why: It's not always better for every property. 1-beds in low-demand markets, properties with recurring maintenance issues, or very low-margin R2R setups may not work. False universalism costs credibility.
rules.alternative: For your specific property, here's the comparison: [specific numbers].

rules.phrase: You don't need to do anything at all
rules.why: Fully managed doesn't mean truly zero involvement. Owners still need to deal with compliance (gas certs, EPC), major maintenance decisions, and insurance. Overclaiming zero effort sets up disappointment.
rules.alternative: Our goal is to make your time involvement as close to zero as possible on the day-to-day. The things that will still need you: major maintenance sign-offs, insurance renewals, compliance certs.

rules.phrase: 70 properties is actually a lot / We're growing very fast
rules.why: Defensiveness about portfolio size is unconvincing. Leads see through it. The size concern is really about accountability quality — address that directly.
rules.alternative: At our size, you get a named person managing your property, not a call centre. That's the trade-off vs a bigger company.

rules.phrase: Just take your time / Let me know when you decide
rules.why: Passive follow-up loses warm leads. After 2-3 weeks without engagement, leads go cold. 'Take your time' is the death of momentum.
rules.alternative: When do you think you'll have that conversation with [partner]? I'll follow up on [specific date] — just to make sure you have everything you need.

rules.phrase: We can definitely get you £X per month
rules.why: Income projections are projections, not guarantees. Using 'definitely' or 'will' instead of 'project' or 'expect' creates liability and trust damage when reality differs.
rules.alternative: Based on comparable properties in your area, we project £X per month in year one.

rules.phrase: [When lead has had damage from previous provider] Oh that shouldn't happen / That's unusual
rules.why: Minimising their experience makes them feel unheard and suggests you don't take damage seriously. Loss-averse-switchers need acknowledgment, not minimisation.
rules.alternative: That's exactly the kind of thing that should have been caught and reported within 24 hours. Here's how our checkout process works.

rules.why: If R2R margin doesn't work, say it clearly. Hedging gives false hope and wastes everyone's time. See entries 024, 031, 035.
rules.alternative: For R2R to work, the rent needs to leave room for management fee, utilities, and profit. At £X rent on this property type, I want to run the numbers honestly before we go further.

rules.why: Some leads specifically ask about VRBO (see entry 050 Helen Bias). Dismissing a platform they know invalidates their research. Explain why Airbnb and Booking.com are prioritised.
rules.alternative: We focus on Airbnb and Booking.com because that's where the volume and review infrastructure lives. VRBO has a smaller UK guest base — we monitor it but it's not a primary channel for UK properties.
